financial.title: Financial Risk
financial.high: You will put your own money on the line — sometimes too readily. Pair appetite with survival conditions so the downside stays survivable.
financial.mid: You selectively accept financial risk, especially for your own venture. You will take a salary cut but think hard before sinking savings.
financial.low: You protect your financial downside. You want predictable conditions before committing money; Venture the risk is funded by others.

reputational.title: Reputational Risk
reputational.high: You will attach your name to a visible stand or an unfinished launch. This builds signal and trust over time — if your judgment is right more often than wrong.
reputational.mid: You accept reputational risk when you believe in the work, but you protect your name where the upside is unclear.
reputational.low: You shield your reputation. Visibility with real downside makes you pull back; useful where trust is your asset, costly where staying silent loses the moment.

relational.title: Relational Risk
relational.high: You will put close relationships on the line for the venture. Loyalty is a real asset; ensure it is not a cost you make someone else pay.
relational.mid: You accept relational risk selectively — with people you trust and on terms you can defend. You avoid asking friends to carry your risk involuntarily.
relational.low: You protect relationships from venture risk. You separate friendship from money; useful for long trust, costly when the venture needs partners from your network.

existential.title: Existential Risk
existential.high: You will bet your career or major life conditions on the venture. This is how rare things happen — and how irreversible mistakes do, too. Name your exit before you arrive.
existential.mid: You accept existential risk only when there is still a path back. You will go far, but not past the point you cannot walk home from.
existential.low: You protect your life conditions. You will not gamble the fallback. This protects durability; it can also mean you never make the bet that needs you fully.

creative.title: Creative Risk
creative.high: You ship before it is finished and change your style for the experiment. Psychological safety with your craft lets you move first and learn faster.
creative.mid: You accept creative risk when the work or the audience can absorb it. You experiment, but you protect the pieces that need precision first.
creative.low: You protect the quality of your craft over speed and novelty. You ship when it is right; valuable where trust depends on it, costly where waiting cedes the field.

shareTexts.invite: What risks would you actually accept?
shareTexts.minimal: I tasted my Risk Palate. What would you accept?

recommendation.label: Outfinity initiative
recommendation.title: Explorer Circle
recommendation.text: Explorer Circle is a community for builders comparing their risk palate and finding partners whose appetites complement rather than clash. You do not need to share your result to participate.
recommendation.note: Opening Explorer Circle takes you to Substack. Your result is not transmitted.
